subject: Re: importing alignments from standalone AreTomo2
body:
Hi Jesse,

I'm also importing alignments from aretomo2. I tried your solution but it 
didn't work very well for me. Do you still have the specific commands you 
used on hand so I can try and follow them?

Thanks,
Daniel
On Monday, October 7, 2024 at 12:27:59 PM UTC-4 Jesse Hansen wrote:

> solved.  setting --alignment_angpix to the unbinned pixel size resolved 
> it.     I had made the mistake that my original stack was aligned using 
> bin8.   
>
> On Monday, October 7, 2024 at 3:58:58 PM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>
>> I meant to add: I am using AreTomo1.3.4 standalone, since that is the ver 
>> I was using when initially processing the 350 TS.
>>
>> On Monday, October 7, 2024 at 3:10:55 PM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>>
>>> Update: I also tried the program ts_import_alignments and it gave a 
>>> similar result: the tomos do not reconstruct well.   
>>>
>>> However, it is possible to import and reconstruct using those same 
>>> alignments into relion without issue.  
>>>
>>> My best guess is due to binning:  Linux Warp exports a binned stack for 
>>> AreTomo to work with, while in the past the stacks from Desktop Warp were 
>>> unbinned and AreTomo worked on that.
>>>
>>> I did the following:
>>>
>>> *Linux Warp: *process frame series as usual. Tilt series alignment 
>>> through AreTomo implemnted through Warp.  This generates a stack (bin 8) 
>>> and tilts file. I use these files as input for AreTomo2 standalone.  The 
>>> resulting tomogram looks great.  I then use ts_import_alignments to import 
>>> the .xf and .tlt, then reconstruct, and the tomogram looks great.   
>>>
>>> *Desktop Warp: *I have previously aligned 350 tilt series in Desktop 
>>> warp (and picked particles, classified, etc.) and would like to import 
>>> those alignments into Linux Warp. Through the Desktop Warp pipeline I 
>>> processed frame series, exported a stack (bin1) and tilts file, ran AreTomo 
>>> on these files and then re-imported into Warp.   Warp then generated 
>>> tomograms that look great.   
>>>
>>> However, if I try to use those output .xf file from this pipeline with 
>>> ts_import_alignments then the tomogram is terrible.  The answer is obvious: 
>>> the bin8 vs bin1 alignments/shifts will be very different in scale.  
>>> However I don't understand .xf files well enough to modify them, because 
>>> it's not simply a matter of multiplying by the difference in binning.  
>>>  Meanwhile, ts_import_alignments seems to be unable to import using .aln 
>>> files, it complains that there is no .xf file.  Is there a way to tell the 
>>> program that it should be looking for .aln rather than .xf?   Not sure if 
>>> this is a bug but I posted on github 
>>> <https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/268> because it seems odd to 
>>> me.  
>>>
>>> another interesting observation:
>>> the original tilts file has the following entries:
>>> -58
>>> -56
>>> -54
>>> -52
>>> ...etc
>>>
>>> while the tilts file from LinuxWarp for the same data is as follows:
>>> -63.98
>>> -61.99
>>> -59.98
>>> -57.99
>>> ...etc
>>>
>>> I'm not sure exactly why this is happening or how to handle it in this 
>>> context.
>>>
>>> Sorry for the long post.  Appreciate any feedback.
>>>
>>> best
>>>
>>> Jesse
>>>     
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Friday, October 4, 2024 at 3:58:27 PM UTC+2 Jesse Hansen wrote:
>>>
>>>> hi all.
>>>>
>>>> For reasons that are very complicated (which I won't get into), I am 
>>>> attempting to import standalone AreTomo2 alignmnets into Warp 2.  
>>>>
>>>> during ts_align I notice that Warp generates the xml, does the 
>>>> alignment (generates aln), then modifies the xml to uploads the 
>>>> rotation/XYshifts.  Ideally I could just swap in my aln file from 
>>>> standalone AreTomo, but there seems to be no way to regenerate the xml file 
>>>> using that aln is input.
>>>>
>>>> Instead I decided to try modifying the xml directly.   For each tilt 
>>>> angle in the xml file I swap in the Rot/TX/TY from the aln file.  I replace 
>>>> the AxisAngle, AxisOffsetX and AxisOffsetY for each tilt.  I notice that 
>>>> the values in Warp are in Ang, while in AreTomo it's in pixels, so I 
>>>> convert in the process.     
>>>>
>>>> However when I try to reconstruct the tomos they look terrible.  Is 
>>>> there something I'm missing?
>>>>
>>>> thanks in advance
>>>>
>>>> Jesse
>>>>
>>>
